title: Linear State-Space System Analysis
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Linear State-Space System Analysis: Linear state-space: recover state x(t) from output y(t) for system dx/dt = Ax + Bu, y = Cx + Du. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.ss.matrices_ABCD…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~50; model_order_mismatch, parameter_perturbation set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: y(t) = C*exp(A*t)*x0 + integral_0 ** t C*exp(A*(t-s))*B*u(s) ds + D*u(t) + n(t)
E.world_state_x: state vector x(t) in R ** n, initial condition x0
E.observation_y: output y(t) in R ** p for p <= n observables

W.regime: Existence of the recovered state_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 50); model_order_mismatch dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: state error decays as exp(-lambda_min(A+LC)*t) with observer gain L. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~50. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |model_order_mismatch|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(n ** 3) for eigenvalue decomposition per iteration
